_comment: RC-0002 — Replication study: same taxpayer, prior year return. Full-salary year with JP Morgan. New features vs RC-0001: high salary (₹19L+), VDA income u/s 115BBH (crypto, 94S TDS), professional tax deduction 16(iii), perquisites ₹6,541, employer TDS computed under old regime with wrong std deduction. Filed within original due date. CPC 143(1) intimation obtained — verified against government computation.

head_1_salary.note: Salary ₹18,96,909 + Perquisites ₹6,541 = ₹19,03,450 gross in Form 16. However CPC accepted taxable salary as ₹18,21,909 (= ₹18,96,909 − ₹75,000 std deduction), meaning perquisites ₹6,541 were NOT included in CPC's salary figure — likely excluded at source or treated separately. Employer computed TDS under old regime, applied std deduction ₹50,000 (wrong — AY2025-26 should be ₹75,000) and 80C ₹57,450 (invalid under new regime). Engine should use correct ₹75,000 regardless of employer's TDS computation.

head_4_capital_gains.description: Virtual Digital Asset income u/s 115BBH — taxed at 30% flat + cess. In ITR-2, VDA flows to capital gains head (line 03 in CPC intimation = ₹3,818). Taxpayer had cost of acquisition — profit determined as ₹3,818 on consideration ₹1,62,608. Tax = ₹1,145 (30% × ₹3,818 + 4% cess).
head_4_capital_gains._rule_gap: RG-003: Section 115BBH not implemented. Engine computes 0 for VDA; actual VDA tax = ₹1,145.

head_5_other_sources.description: Prior year 244A refund interest — shown in prefill incDeductionsOthIncCPC as ₹1,743 (AY2024-25 refund interest). Taxable as other sources.

tds_credits_from_26AS.employer: Crypto Platform (anonymized) — VDA TDS at 1% on ₹1,62,608 consideration

human_expected._comment: New regime. CPC-verified figures from 143(1) intimation. Salary (CPC): ₹18,21,909 (= ₹18,96,909 − ₹75,000 std deduction; perquisites ₹6,541 excluded by CPC). Capital gains (VDA): ₹3,818. Other sources: ₹2,243 (SAV ₹496 + DIV ₹4 + prior year refund interest ₹1,743). Chapter VI-A deduction (80CCD(2) employer NPS): ₹70,000. Taxable income = ₹18,21,909 + ₹3,818 + ₹2,243 − ₹70,000 = ₹17,57,970. Tax: slab tax on ₹17,54,152 (excl VDA) + VDA tax ₹1,145 + surcharge ₹0 + cess 4% = ₹2,26,087. TDS ₹3,58,652 + 244A interest ₹9,938 = refund ₹1,42,500.

notes: HIGH SALARY YEAR. Full-time employment at JP Morgan throughout the year. CPC 143(1) intimation obtained for AY2025-26 — all figures government-verified. Key differences from RC-0001 and engine rule gaps: (1) Single employer with large salary — tests high-income slab computation and regime comparison at ₹19L+ income. (2) VDA income (crypto): Neblio Technologies paid ₹1,62,608 consideration under 94S at 1% TDS (₹1,633). VDA PROFIT WAS KNOWN — ₹3,818 (not null as initially recorded). Section 115BBH taxes VDA at 30% flat + cess with NO deductions except cost of acquisition. Tax = ₹1,145. In ITR-2, VDA flows to capital gains head (line 03 in intimation). RG-003: Engine 115BBH not implemented — engine computes ₹0 for VDA. (3) Professional tax ₹2,400 u/s 16(iii): deductible under both regimes. RG-004: Engine does not implement 16(iii). Note: taxpayer also did NOT claim this deduction in the filed ITR (Form 24Q shows 16iii = ₹2,400 but ITR computation did not include it — CPC accepted without this deduction). (4) Perquisites ₹6,541: included in gross salary per Form 16 (₹18,96,909 + ₹6,541 = ₹19,03,450) but CPC's salary figure is ₹18,21,909 = ₹18,96,909 − ₹75,000, meaning perquisites were NOT included in the CPC computation. Engine will include them — overstating salary by ₹6,541. (5) Employer NPS 80CCD(2): ₹70,000 employer NPS contribution claimed and accepted by CPC. RG-005: Engine does not implement 80CCD(2) — understates deductions by ₹70,000. (6) Prior year refund interest ₹1,743 (244A from AY2024-25): taxable as other sources. Shown in prefill incDeductionsOthIncCPC. Engine case did not initially include this. (7) Employer TDS error: employer applied std deduction ₹50,000 (wrong for AY2025-26) and 80C ₹57,450 (invalid under new regime). Engine correctly applies ₹75,000. (8) 244A interest ₹9,938 added to refund (6% on ₹1,32,570 refund for ~15 months). (9) CPC waived 234C interest of ₹132 → total interest = ₹0. TOTAL TDS ₹3,58,652.

cpc_intimation.cpc_adjustments: No adjustments to declared income or deductions. All figures accepted as filed. 234C interest waived from ₹132 to ₹0.

defects_found: D-002: r0022_interest_234abc read tds_credit_from_26AS (scalar key, never populated) instead of summing tds_credits_from_26AS (list). For RC-0001 this was hidden because total_tax=0 so 0>=0.90*0. RC-0002 has total_tax=₹2,48,232 so the wrong tds=0 caused engine to charge ₹19,982 interest (234B) instead of 0. Fix: sum items from tds_credits_from_26AS. 13 GMs regenerated. closure_stage: Verified (RC-0002 confirmed fix).

D-003: wave3a.py other_total sum used item.get('amount', 0) which returns None when 'amount': null is present in JSON (dict.get default only applies when key is absent, not when value is None). VDA entry with null amount caused TypeError. Fix: use item.get('amount') or 0. closure_stage: Verified (RC-0002 confirmed fix).

D-004 (side-find): T-0043 unit test used tds_credit_from_26AS (singular scalar, pre-D-002 key) — test was silently broken after D-002 fix. Found during RG-005 implementation when running full test suite. Fix: updated T-0043 to use tds_credits_from_26AS list format. closure_stage: Verified (107/107 pass).
rule_gaps_observed: RG-003: Section 115BBH (VDA income) not implemented — engine computes 0 for VDA income; actual VDA profit ₹3,818, tax ₹1,145. VDA flows to capital gains head in ITR-2. closure_stage: Explained.

RG-004: Section 16(iii) professional tax deduction not implemented — engine over-reports taxable salary by ₹2,400. Note: taxpayer also did not claim this in the filed ITR; CPC accepted without it. closure_stage: Explained.

RG-005: Section 80CCD(2) employer NPS not implemented — engine misses ₹70,000 deduction that is valid under new regime (115BAC allows 80CCD(2)). IMPLEMENTED in commit 4f8ea12 via R-0041. Engine taxable_income_new_regime reduced by ₹64,439 (₹70,000 deduction − ₹3,818 VDA and ₹1,743 interest now correctly included). closure_stage: Implemented. Awaiting Verified (needs independent case exercising 80CCD(2)).
RG-001: Section 288A rounding not applied — minor delta expected. closure_stage: Explained.
RG-002: demand_amount not produced — engine returns None instead of 0. closure_stage: Explained.

delta_analysis.taxable_income_new_regime: Engine ₹18,28,950 vs CPC ₹17,57,970 — delta +₹70,980 decomposes as: +₆,541 (perquisites included by engine, excluded by CPC) +₇0,000 (80CCD(2) employer NPS missing in engine — RG-005) −₃,818 (VDA income missing in engine — RG-003) −₁,743 (prior year refund interest missing from initial case evidence). Net: 6541+70000−3818−1743 = +70980 ✓
delta_analysis.total_tax_new_regime: Engine ₹2,48,232 vs CPC ₹2,26,087 — delta driven by taxable income differences above plus missing VDA tax ₹1,145 (RG-003) and missing 80CCD(2) deduction (RG-005).
delta_analysis.refund_amount: Engine ₹1,10,420 vs CPC ₹1,42,500 — delta ₹32,080. Engine missing 244A interest credit ₹9,938 and over-computing tax by ₹22,145 (taxable income error).
delta_analysis.total_interest: 0 — matches CPC (D-002 fix applied; return filed within due date; TDS ₹3,58,652 > 90% of tax ₂,48,232). CPC also waived 234C ₹132.
